channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fires in Cape Town Informal Settlements Leave Over 500 People Displaced
item.description: More than 500 residents have been forced to leave their homes after fires broke out in the Imizamo Yethu and Masiphumelele informal settlements in Cape Town. Preliminary assessments indicate that roug...

item.title: Finance Minister calls on banks to actively support Tanzania Development Vision 2050
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Finance Minister, Ambassador Khamis Mussa Omar, has called on all financial institutions in Tanzania to actively support the Tanzania Development Vision 2050, underlining the impor...

item.title: Tanzanian Government Provides TZS 10 Million Aid to Veteran Musician Zahir Zorro
item.description: The Tanzanian government has shown compassion by handing over TZS 10 million in relief assistance to veteran musician Zahir Ally Zorro, who is currently battling health challenges. Zahir Zorro, a lege...

item.title: Solar power helps Laikipia dairy farmers cut costs and increase milk production
item.description: In Laikipia County, the Solio Ranch Settlement Farmers Cooperative Society (SRSFCS) was struggling with high electricity bills and frequent blackouts that negatively affected milk production and farme...

item.title: Poor Diet and Lack of Physical Activity Increasing Diabetes Cases Among Nigerian Children, Says Paediatric Endocrinologist
item.description: Diabetes, a chronic condition affecting blood sugar processing, no longer dey only for adults as Nigerian pikin dey now face higher risk due to unhealthy diets high in sugar and processed foods, plus ...

item.title: Indian Embassy Confirms No Indian Casualties After Projectile Hits Residential Area in Al Kharj
item.description: The Indian Embassy in Riyadh has clarified that there were no Indian fatalities after a military projectile, reportedly from Iran, struck a residential building in Al Kharj, Saudi Arabia. One Indian n...

item.title: Strong Winds and Snow Cause Power Outages Across Alberta
item.description: Environment Canada has issued warnings for strong winds in parts of Alberta, with gusts reaching between 90 and 110 km/h, causing damage to trees, roofs, fences, and other structures. High-sided vehic...

item.title: Key Developments on Day 10 of the US-Israel Conflict with Iran
item.description: On the 10th day of the escalating conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran, significant developments have reshaped the regional and global landscape. Mojtaba Khamenei has been named Iran’s new suprem...

item.title: State Bank of Pakistan keeps key policy rate steady at 10.5pc amid economic pressures
item.description: On Monday, the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) decided to maintain its key policy rate at 10.5 per cent, as announced on the central bank's X account. Analysts were largely expecting this decision, notin...

item.title: KSE-100 suffers major fall amid geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices
item.description: On Monday, Pakistan’s benchmark KSE-100 index experienced a severe fall, losing 11,015.96 points, equivalent to almost 7 per cent, and closing at 146,480.14 points. Trading volume was recorded at over...

item.title: NDLEA Arrests Ex-Lagos Councillor and Others in Major Drug Seizures Across Nigeria
item.description: The NDLEA don carry out serious operations across Nigeria as dem arrest former Lagos councillor, Sheleru Sadiq Olalekan, for having 40 kilograms of skunk for him house in Ilado. Sheleru, wey don becom...

item.title: Somali President Participates in EAC Summit in Arusha to Promote Regional Cooperation
item.description: Somalia President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud attended the 25th Ordinary Summit of Heads of State of the East African Community (EAC) in Arusha, Tanzania, where leaders from the region discussed ways to str...

item.title: BuCor Director General Catapang Files Perjury and Cyberlibel Complaints Against 18 Alleged Former Marines and Lawyer Levi Baligod
item.description: Bureau of Corrections (BuCor) Director General Gregorio Catapang Jr. filed perjury and cyberlibel complaints on Monday with the Department of Justice (DOJ) against 18 individuals claiming to be former...

item.title: One person dies and three injured in two separate Katsina highway accidents
item.description: On Sunday for Katsina State, two separate auto accidents happen along the Dayi–Malumfashi road, and one person lose him life while three others suffer serious injuries. The first accident occur early ...

item.title: Sindh Government Plans Cabinet Meeting to Discuss Fuel Rationing and Conservation Measures Amid Middle East Crisis
item.description: The Sindh government is taking serious steps to handle a possible fuel crisis because of the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran, which has led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. This key route is very...

item.title: Graham Nash Criticizes Trump’s Approach to Iran Conflict
item.description: Graham Nash, the renowned singer-songwriter of Crosby, Stills & Nash and The Hollies, publicly criticized President Donald Trump for his aggressive stance toward Iran. Posting on Instagram, Nash conde...

item.title: Government Introduces Fees for Visiting the Twelve Apostles in Victoria
item.description: The Victorian government has announced that visitors will now have to pay an entry fee to see the iconic Twelve Apostles along the Great Ocean Road. The move has sparked immediate backlash, with criti...

item.title: Palace explains President Marcos’ UN trip despite global oil concerns
item.description: Malacañang defended President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr.'s working visit to New York City amid the ongoing Middle East tensions, which have impacted oil prices in the Philippines. The President’s...

item.title: Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos reportedly end engagement before wedding
item.description: Dallas Cowboys quarterback Dak Prescott and his fiancée, Sarah Jane Ramos, have reportedly ended their engagement just weeks before their planned wedding. According to insiders, the couple had been ex...

item.title: Ryan Nembhard Develops Under Guidance of NBA Veteran Jason Kidd
item.description: Ryan Nembhard, the young Canadian basketball player from Aurora, Ontario, is making strides in his NBA career with the Dallas Mavericks. Coming from a basketball-oriented family—his father Claude is C...
